title: NOC 40021 – School Principals and Administrators of Elementary and Secondary Education | GTR Immigration
description: School Principals and Administrators of Elementary and Secondary Education under NOC 40021 are senior leaders responsible for managing academic institutions and school districts across Canada. Classified as TEER 0 (Management Occupation), this role represents one of the highest leadership levels within Canada’s K-12 education system. With Canada’s growing student population, curriculum modernization, and ongoing education reforms, experienced school administrators remain in steady demand nationwide.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category4 – Occupations in education, law, and social, community, and government servicesTEER0 – Management occupationsMajor Group40 – Managers in public administration and educationSub-Major Group400 – Managers in public administration and educationMinor Group4002 – Managers in educationNOC Code40021

duties: Plan, organize, direct, and evaluate school activities
Administrators of Elementary and Secondary Education
Oversee academic affairs of a school district
Supervise recruitment and evaluation of teaching staff
employment_requirements: Master’s degree in education may be required
Several years of experience as a senior teacher or department head
Supervisory officer certificate may be required for district-level administrators
Leadership certification requirements vary by province.
salary_prospects: Annual earnings typically range from $64,500 to $124,900, depending on province, seniority, and district size.
The outlook for NOC 40021 is stable, supported by:
Leadership replacement demand due to retirements
Experienced educators transitioning into administration may find strong long-term career prospects.
labour_market_demand: Professionals under NOC 40021 are employed in:
Strong opportunities exist in provinces such as Ontario, Alberta, British Columbia, Quebec, Saskatchewan, Manitoba, and northern jurisdictions like Nunavut and Northwest Territories.
Leadership roles within Canada’s public education system
Professionals with extensive teaching and supervisory experience are particularly competitive.
